things.name: Bahai Gardens and Shrine (UNESCO)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage eighteen terraced gardens cascading down Mount Carmel with the golden-domed Shrine of the Bab; among the most breathtaking landscaped gardens on earth

things.name: German Colony
things.description: Beautifully preserved 19th-century Templar colony with stone buildings, boutique restaurants and the Ben Gurion Boulevard; the most atmospheric neighbourhood in Haifa

things.name: Haifa Museum of Art
things.description: Major collection of Israeli and international modern art with an outstanding focus on contemporary Israeli artists; housed in a striking building in the Hadar district

things.name: Stella Maris Carmelite Monastery
things.description: Spectacular 19th-century monastery atop Mount Carmel with a lighthouse and panoramic Mediterranean views; an important Christian pilgrimage site revered by Carmelite monks

things.name: Clandestine Immigration and Naval Museum
things.description: Absorbing museum in the German Colony telling the story of illegal Jewish immigration to British Mandate Palestine before the founding of Israel in 1948

things.name: Carmel Market (HaCarmel)
things.description: Haifa's vibrant open-air market on the slopes of Mount Carmel with fresh produce, Middle Eastern spices, hummus stalls and the smells and sounds of Israeli street life

things.name: Haifa Cable Car
things.description: Aerial cable car connecting the lower German Colony to the upper Carmel neighbourhood with sweeping views over Haifa Bay; a quick and scenic way to traverse the steep hillside

things.name: Technion — Israel Institute of Technology
things.description: Israel's MIT and one of the world's top technical universities; the striking campus on Mount Carmel and the Tech Museum chart Israel's extraordinary innovation story

things.name: Acre (Akko) Old City (UNESCO)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage Crusader city just 20km north of Haifa with Ottoman Khan al-Umdan, underground Crusader halls, vibrant Arab market and legendary sea walls

things.name: Sammy Ofer Stadium
things.description: Modern 30,820-seat stadium shared by Maccabi Haifa and Hapoel Haifa; Maccabi Haifa made a remarkable run to the Champions League group stage in 2022-23
